title: France Just Broke Its All-Time Heat Record. 40 Drowned.
script: France just hit 44°C! That's not a typo. On June 23, France recorded its hottest day in history — 44.3 degrees Celsius, or about 112 degrees Fahrenheit, in parts of the southwest. Météo France put 54 departments — more than half the country — under a red heatwave alert. Now here's the worst part. At least 40 people drowned in rivers and lakes, trying to escape the heat. A 13-year-old girl drowned in the Seine. A professional footballer was pulled from the Rhône in critical condition. 2 young children were found dead in a parked car in southern France. This is not an ordinary heat wave. France has seen records broken year after year — but this one, June 23, 2026, is the worst in recorded French history. How many more summers like this can Europe handle?
